summary.intent: Implement CHA-35: automated cron-based follow-up engine that periodically queries the CRM for stale contacts and fires an agent turn to notify the user, mirroring the existing heartbeat job registration pattern.
summary.outcome: All 10 target files were modified or created (config schema, validation, template, CRM store trait + SQLite impl, gateway follow_up module, server wiring, and state struct). Compilation was initiated but the transcript ends before confirming a clean build, open PR, or passing test suite.

repo: Heartbeat job registration at server.rs:4925-5035 is the canonical pattern for system cron jobs: idempotent upsert with a fixed __name__ ID, config-driven enable/disable.
CRM PII fields (email, phone) are wrapped in Secret<String>; the agreed convention is to never expose them to LLM prompts — use a StaleContactSummary projection type instead.
The data retention engine (retention.rs) is a detached tokio::spawn loop, NOT a cron job — contrasts with heartbeat/follow-up which use CronService.
GatewayInner in state.rs holds per-feature config copies (e.g. heartbeat_config); follow_up_config was added following the same field pattern.
CronService uses CronSchedule::Cron { expr } for calendar-aware schedules vs CronSchedule::Every { every_ms } used by heartbeat.
The on_agent_turn callback detects special sessions via SessionTarget::Named; a shared const for '__follow_up__' avoids typo risk between registration and detection sites.
DRY extraction of a register_system_job() helper was explicitly deferred until a third system job exists (currently heartbeat + follow_up = two instances).

code.finding: Heartbeat job registration block — exact pattern copied for follow-up registration.
GatewayInner struct holds heartbeat_config; follow_up_config added alongside it.
CrmStore trait extended with contacts_needing_followup(stale_days, limit) returning Vec<StaleContactInfo>; MemoryStore gets a default fallback impl via pagination.
SQL impl uses a single LEFT JOIN query to find contacts with no recent interaction, avoiding N+1. A local row type was added for sqlx mapping.
New module: StaleContactSummary (PII-safe projection), build_followup_prompt(), and DEFAULT_STALE_DAYS constant shared between registration and detection.

workflow: The agent ran cargo check asynchronously at the end but the transcript ends before results were available — build verification was incomplete.
Multiple sequential server.rs reads were required to find exact insertion points, suggesting large files benefit from targeted grep-then-read rather than full reads.
The agent attempted to use ToolSearch and TaskOutput to retrieve the background cargo check result, indicating background tasks need explicit polling hooks.

Linear comments served as a persistent workpad across investigation and implementation stages, carrying architecture decisions (prompt enrichment vs agent tools, stale_days heuristic, notify-only mode, PII exclusion) forward into implementation.

friction: cargo check was launched as a background task but results were not confirmed before the session ended — build status is unknown.
server.rs is very large (5000+ lines), requiring multiple partial reads with offset/limit to locate exact insertion points for the follow-up job registration and on_agent_turn enrichment blocks.
The crm crate needed a new `time` dependency added to Cargo.toml before the stale contact query could use timestamp arithmetic — discovered mid-implementation.
The GatewayInner state struct had to be found via grep before follow_up_config could be added, as it wasn't immediately obvious from the server.rs entry point.
No PR was created and no tests were run before the session ended; the quality bar checklist (tests pass, no type errors, no lint errors) was not verified.
open_items: Confirm cargo check / cargo test passes cleanly — build verification was incomplete at session end.
Create the GitHub PR and link it to Linear issue CHA-35.
Write the 14 unit/integration tests identified in the engineering review (T1-T14), especially T8 (PII exclusion — marked CRITICAL).
Stage-aware follow-up thresholds (different stale_days per contact stage) — explicitly deferred to a future PR.
Autonomous outreach mode — deferred pending product/legal decision.
Follow-up outcome tracking (new CRM interaction types) — deferred.
FOLLOW_UP.md prompt file (mirroring HEARTBEAT.md) — nice-to-have, not implemented.
Extract register_system_job() helper when a third system cron job is added (currently two: heartbeat + follow-up).
Consider migrating the data retention engine from tokio::spawn to a CronService job — noted as out of scope but flagged as technical debt.
